description: A candid color photograph taken inside what appears to be a manufacturing/upholstery workshop or warehouse, with shelving full of boxes and materials and fluorescent lighting overhead. In the foreground a seated older Black woman wearing glasses, gray locs, and a bright yellow/gold t-shirt (with a small logo) looks up and smiles toward a woman standing on the right - a white woman with long dark hair, glasses, and a light blue blazer (consistent with Labor Secretary appearance) who is examining a green floral fabric. At the far left a videographer in a blue shirt films the interaction with a professional camera. The image has no on-image text, seal, or graphic design - it is a documentary photo of an official facility visit. Marked is_designed=false. The tweet supplies the messaging: 'We'll never give up our mission. AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST!' framing the visit as the administration championing workers. Target audience: general public / worker constituency.
notable_text: (none on image; tweet text: 'We'll never give up our mission. AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST!')
